CUI Boundary Classification — Acme Defense LLC

DoD CMMC Asset Taxonomy Applied:

CUI Assets (in scope):
- Engineering workstations (12): CUI asset — directly process and store CUI technical drawings; in scope
- File server FS-01: CUI asset — stores CUI drawings and specifications; FS-01 is in scope for CMMC Level 2 assessment
- Microsoft 365 GCC High: CUI asset — GCC High email and SharePoint handle CUI collaboration; GCC High in scope for CMMC Level 2

Security Protection Assets (in scope):
- Jump server JMP-01: security protection asset — provides admin access to CUI systems; must be included in assessment scope
- Managed SOC provider: security protection asset — SOC provider receives log feeds from CUI systems; SOC provider requires DFARS 252.204-7012 flow-down assessment as an External Service Provider (ESP/MSP)

Contractor Risk Managed Assets:
- Azure DevOps (commercial): contractor risk managed — internal tooling only; no CUI present; out-of-scope or limited-scope assessment

Out of Scope:
- HR server HR-01: out of scope — employee records only; no CUI access or processing
- IT Help Desk system: out of scope — no CUI access
- Salesforce CRM: out of scope — no CUI data
- Payroll processor: out of scope — payroll data only; no CUI

Key Scoping Determinations: M365 GCC High in scope — CUI resides in SharePoint and email. Commercial Azure DevOps is out of scope or contractor risk managed — no CUI present. MSP/SOC provider flow-down under DFARS 7012 must be confirmed before assessment closes.

Subcontractor DFARS Flow-Down Obligations — Tier-2 CUI Handler

Applicable clauses: DFARS 252.204-7012 (Safeguarding), 252.204-7019 (SPRS Reporting), and 252.204-7021 (CMMC) — all three flow down and apply equally to you as a tier-2 subcontractor.

As a tier-2 subcontractor handling CUI for Lockheed Martin, all three DFARS clauses flow down and apply equally:

1. DFARS 252.204-7012 (Safeguarding CUI): You must implement all 110 NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 practices for any system that handles, stores, or transmits CUI. The clause flows down regardless of subcontract tier — 7012 applies to you the same as to a direct contractor.

2. DFARS 252.204-7019 (SPRS Reporting): You must submit a current SPRS self-assessment score to the Supplier Performance Risk System database. SPRS must be submitted and current before you begin CUI handling. The prime (Lockheed) must verify your SPRS score in the database prior to contract performance.

3. DFARS 252.204-7021 (CMMC Requirement): You must achieve the CMMC Level specified in the prime's contract. 7021 flows down to all tiers that touch CUI — tier-2 status does not reduce this obligation.

SPRS Obligation:
SPRS score must be submitted to the SPRS database and reflect a current self-assessment (within 3 years). Lockheed Martin must verify your SPRS submission exists and confirm the score before allowing CUI access. The prime may require contractual attestation in addition to the SPRS database check.

Tier-2 Distinction:
Your tier-2 status does not change your obligations. DFARS flow-down provisions apply regardless of tier — any subcontractor handling CUI under a DoD contract chain has the same obligation as a direct prime. CUI handling triggers the requirements irrespective of whether you are tier-1, tier-2, or deeper. The obligation is the same at every tier.

POA&M Table — Acme Defense LLC CMMC Level 2 OTS Findings

Findings covered: 3.1.3 (AC — DLP), 3.5.3 (IA — MFA), 3.3.1 (AU — log retention), 3.13.3 (SC — PAW), 3.14.1 (SI — vuln scan)

| Practice ID | Domain | Gap Description | Remediation Action | Responsible Party | Target Completion Date |
|-------------|--------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------------|----------------------|
| 3.1.3 | AC | No DLP solution; CUI can egress via email without controls; email gateway logs confirm no DLP policy or enforcement | Deploy email-gateway DLP controls to block CUI egress; configure data classification rules for CUI content | IT Security | 2026-09-30 |
| 3.5.3 | IA | MFA enforced for O365 only; on-premises CUI systems use password-only; AD GPO export confirms no local MFA | Enable two-factor MFA on all CUI system logons via Azure AD MFA or hardware token; update GPO and policy | IT Security | 2026-08-31 |
| 3.3.1 | AU | Audit log retention set to 30 days; 90-day minimum required; Windows Event Viewer config confirms 30-day policy | Configure centralized log retention to 90 days on all CUI systems; evaluate SIEM for central log aggregation | IT Operations | 2026-07-31 |
| 3.13.3 | SC | System admin functions accessible from same workstations used for CUI work; no PAW policy | Implement privileged access PAW workstations for all admin roles; enforce separation via policy and technical controls | IT Security | 2026-10-31 |
| 3.14.1 | SI | No formal vulnerability scanning program; ad hoc patching only; no scan reports available | Install automated scan tooling (Tenable or Qualys) and establish patch management SLA with tracked remediation | IT Operations | 2026-09-30 |

To perform a CMMC 2.0 gap analysis, a System Security Plan (SSP) is required. Without an SSP or equivalent artifact documentation, I cannot assess your controls, identify gaps, or compute an SPRS score.

Please provide your SSP or a control implementation summary before we begin.

What I need before proceeding:
1. System Security Plan (SSP) — documenting implemented NIST SP 800-171 controls with artifact evidence
2. Asset inventory identifying which systems handle, store, or transmit CUI
3. Any prior assessment results, audit findings, or SPRS submissions

I cannot produce a gap list, domain verdicts, or SPRS assessment without this documentation. Any score generated without an SSP would be speculative and unsuitable for DFARS compliance purposes. Please provide the SSP so the assessment can proceed.

SPRS Score Calculation — 50 Not Met Practices

Baseline: 110 (DoD Assessment Methodology)

High-severity (5-point) Not Met practices identified:
- 3.14.1 (SI) — identify and correct system flaws
- 3.14.2 (SI) — provide malicious code protection
- 3.14.4 (SI) — update malicious code protections
- 3.1.2 (AC) — limit system access types and functions
- 3.1.3 (AC) — control CUI flow between networks
- 3.1.5 (AC) — employ principle of least privilege
Subtotal: 6 practices × 5 points = 30 points deducted

Medium-severity (3-point) Not Met practices (22 practices across RA, CA, MP, PS, PE, MA, AT, remaining SI):
Subtotal: 22 × 3 = 66 points deducted

Low-severity (1-point) Not Met practices (remaining 22 practices):
Subtotal: 22 × 1 = 22 points deducted

Estimated SPRS score: 110 − 30 − 66 − 22 = SPRS -8
Note: Exact score requires mapping each practice ID to official DoD severity weights; plausible range is -90 to +20 depending on assignments.

System and Information Integrity (SI): Not Met across all 7 practices — highest concentration of 5-point weight items; address first.
Risk Assessment (RA): all 3 practices not met — ongoing risk assessment program absent.

Top 5 prioritized remediation items (highest SPRS point recovery per practice):
1. First priority: 3.14.1 (SI) — 5-point item; flaw identification program entirely absent; high exploitability risk
2. Second: 3.1.2 (AC) — 5-point; access type restrictions not enforced; lateral movement risk
3. Third: 3.14.2 (SI) — 5-point; malicious code protection gaps across all SI systems
4. Fourth: 3.1.3 (AC) — 5-point; CUI flow control not implemented
5. Fifth: 3.1.5 (AC) — 5-point; least privilege not enforced organization-wide

C3PAO Assessment Preparation Checklist — 90-Day Plan

Your SPRS score of -12 indicates approximately 122 points of deductions above the 110 baseline. The C3PAO assessor team (DIBCAC-accredited) will apply NIST SP 800-171A assessment methodology: Examine, Interview, and Test methods.

Assessment Methodology (800-171A):
- Examine: Document review — policy, SSP, procedures, configuration baseline records (evidence of implementation)
- Interview: Personnel discussions — proof of organizational awareness and role-based control knowledge
- Test: Technical verification — operational proof of control function through active testing

Evidence Package Summary by Domain:
AC — GPO export for access control policy; user access review records; privileged account inventory listing AC controls
IA — MFA config and authentication log showing IA enforcement; service account inventory; AD password policy GPO
CM — configuration baseline config documents for CUI systems; software inventory; change management log
AU — audit log retention policy; centralized log management config; sample log export proving 90-day retention
SC — firewall baseline config and network diagram; segmentation scan results showing SC boundary controls
SI — vulnerability scan reports (last 30 days); patch log and flaw remediation policy for SI practices
IR — current IR plan; tabletop exercise record; CISA/US-CERT reporting procedures
RA — formal risk assessment (within 12 months); risk register
CA — security assessment plan and results; POA&M tracking open items
MP — removable media policy; media sanitization log
PS — background check records; personnel screening policy
PE — physical access control log; visitor records; CUI storage area designation
MA — maintenance log for CUI systems; remote maintenance procedures
AT — security awareness training records; CUI-specific training completion log

SPRS Remediation Strategy (from SPRS -12 to positive territory):
Your SPRS -12 score means you have deductions totaling approximately 122 points. Focus on the 5-point weight practices first — each remediated 5 point highest-weight OTS item recovers 5 SPRS points. Remediating 3–5 of the highest weight 5-point practices could shift SPRS into positive territory before the C3PAO assessment.

Assessment prep checklist before C3PAO assessment:
- [ ] Update SSP to reflect current control implementation
- [ ] Complete evidence packages for all 14 domains (see above)
- [ ] Close highest-priority 5-point OTS findings
- [ ] Conduct internal mock review using 800-171A Examine/Interview/Test methods
- [ ] Verify current SPRS self-assessment is posted in SPRS database
- [ ] Confirm POA&M is current and tracks all open items
